    <h1 {...{
      "id": "user-interfaces"
    }}>{`User Interfaces`}</h1>
    <p>{`Learn how to create beautiful and functional user interfaces for your UXP plugins`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`Every UXP plugin with a visual component needs a user interface. Whether you're building a simple dialog or a complex panel, UXP gives you multiple ways to create UI that looks and feels like a native part of Premiere.`}</p>
    <p>{`UXP provides three approaches for building user interfaces:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "#standard-html-elements"
        }}><strong parentName="a">{`Standard HTML Elements`}</strong></a>{`: familiar web technologies with custom styling.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "#spectrum-uxp-widgets"
        }}><strong parentName="a">{`Spectrum UXP Widgets`}</strong></a>{`: built-in, Adobe-styled components that work out of the box.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "#spectrum-web-components-swc"
        }}><strong parentName="a">{`Spectrum Web Components (SWC)`}</strong></a>{`: modern Web Component library with Adobe's design system.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Recommended approach`}</p>
    <p>{`Each technology has its strengths and you can mix and match them in the same plugin—although `}<strong parentName="p">{`we recommend using Spectrum Web Components`}</strong>{` going forward for the most complete feature set and future support. Please read the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-spectrum/index.md"
      }}>{`Spectrum UXP Reference`}</a>{` for more details.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "understand-the-options"
    }}>{`Understand the Options`}</h2>
    <p>{`Before diving into the technical details, it's helpful to understand some key terminology:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://spectrum.adobe.com/"
        }}><strong parentName="a">{`Spectrum`}</strong></a>{`: Adobe's open-source design language`}<sup parentName="li" {...{
          "id": "fnref-1"
        }}><a parentName="sup" {...{
            "href": "#fn-1",
            "className": "footnote-ref"
          }}>{`1`}</a></sup>{` and guidelines that ensure consistency across applications.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Web_components"
        }}><strong parentName="a">{`Web Components`}</strong></a>{`: A set of HTML5 technologies that let you create custom, reusable HTML elements.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://opensource.adobe.com/spectrum-web-components/"
        }}><strong parentName="a">{`Adobe Spectrum Web Components`}</strong></a>{` (SWC): An open-source library of pre-built Web Components styled according to Spectrum design guidelines.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "standard-html-elements"
    }}>{`Standard HTML Elements`}</h3>
    <p>{`These are the familiar HTML elements you've likely used before: `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<div>`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<button>`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<input>`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<img>`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<dialog>`}</inlineCode>{`, and more. They follow web standards and give you complete control over styling through CSS.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<div class="container">
  <button class="primary-button">Click me</button>
  <input type="text" placeholder="Enter text" />
</div>
`}</code></pre>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Best for`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Trade-offs`}</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Building `}<strong parentName="td">{`highly customized interfaces`}</strong>{` that need match different design systems`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Complex and expensive to implement`}</td>
        </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`UXP is not a browser`}</p>
    <p>{`While UXP supports modern web technologies, it's not a full browser environment. Not all HTML elements, CSS properties, or JavaScript APIs available in browsers will work in UXP. Check the list of `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-html/General/Unsupported%20Elements.md"
      }}>{`unsupported HTML elements`}</a>{` and `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-html/General/Unsupported%20Attributes.md"
      }}>{`unsupported attributes`}</a>{` for details.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "spectrum-uxp-widgets"
    }}>{`Spectrum UXP Widgets`}</h3>
    <p>{`Spectrum UXP Widgets are built directly into the UXP platform. They provide ready-to-use, Adobe-styled components that automatically match Premiere's look and feel, including dark and light theme support.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<sp-button variant="primary">I'm a Spectrum button</sp-button>
<sp-textfield placeholder="Enter your name"></sp-textfield>
<sp-checkbox>Enable feature</sp-checkbox>
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`These widgets are immediately available—no installation or imports required. Just use them like any other HTML tag.`}</p>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Best for`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Trade-offs`}</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`Quick prototyping`}</strong>{`, getting started with UXP, or when you want Premiere's native look without extra setup.`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`Limited number of components`}</strong>{` available. `}<br /><strong parentName="td">{`You can't customize their behavior`}</strong>{` beyond the provided API or easily inspect their internal structure.`}</td>
        </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "spectrum-web-components-swc"
    }}>{`Spectrum Web Components (SWC)`}</h3>
    <p><a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://opensource.adobe.com/spectrum-web-components/"
      }}>{`Spectrum Web Components`}</a>{` are Adobe's official implementation of the Spectrum Design System using modern `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Web_components"
      }}>{`Web Component standards`}</a>{`. They offer the most complete feature set and the closest adherence to Spectrum guidelines.`}</p>
    <p>{`To use SWCs, you need to `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://opensource.adobe.com/spectrum-web-components/getting-started/"
      }}>{`install each component`}</a>{` individually via `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`npm`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`yarn`}</inlineCode>{` and import it in your code:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-bash"
      }}>{`npm install @spectrum-web-components/button@0.37.0
npm install @spectrum-web-components/textfield@0.37.0
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Version Requirement`}</p>
    <p>{`For Premiere plugins, `}<strong parentName="p">{`all Spectrum Web Components must be locked to version 0.37.0`}</strong>{` for the time being. This ensures compatibility with the current UXP version.`}</p>
    <p>{`Then import and use them in your JavaScript:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`import '@spectrum-web-components/button/sp-button.js';
import '@spectrum-web-components/textfield/sp-textfield.js';
`}</code></pre>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<sp-button variant="primary">I'm a SWC button</sp-button>
<sp-textfield placeholder="Enter your name"></sp-textfield>
`}</code></pre>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Best for`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Trade-offs`}</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`Production plugins`}</strong>{` that need the `}<strong parentName="td">{`full range of Spectrum components`}</strong>{`, or when you need to inspect and debug component internals.`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Requires Node.js, a package manager (npm/yarn), and a bundler (Webpack, Rollup, etc.) to build your plugin.`}</td>
        </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
    <p>{`You'll also need to enable SWC in your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "featureFlags": {
    "enableSWCSupport": true
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "mix-and-match"
    }}>{`Mix and Match`}</h2>
    <p>{`The best part? You can combine all three approaches in a single plugin. UXP seamlessly supports mixing HTML elements, Spectrum UXP Widgets, and Spectrum Web Components:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<form>
  <!-- Standard HTML element -->
  <div class="section">
    <!-- Spectrum Web Component -->
    <sp-banner>
      <div slot="header">Welcome</div>
      <div slot="content">This is a mixed UI example</div>
    </sp-banner>

    <!-- Spectrum UXP Widget -->
    <sp-button variant="primary">Submit</sp-button>
  </div>
</form>
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`This flexibility lets you use the right tool for each part of your interface.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "which-approach-should-you-choose"
    }}>{`Which Approach Should You Choose?`}</h2>
    <p>{`Your choice depends on your project requirements, timeline, and experience level.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "start-with-spectrum-uxp-widgets-if-you"
    }}>{`Start with Spectrum UXP Widgets if you:`}</h4>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Are new to UXP development`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Want to quickly prototype an idea`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Need a simple UI with standard components`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Prefer zero configuration`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "use-spectrum-web-components-if-you"
    }}>{`Use Spectrum Web Components if you:`}</h4>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Are building a production plugin`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Need access to the full Spectrum component library`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Want to inspect and debug component internals`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Are comfortable with npm and build tools`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "use-standard-html-elements-if-you"
    }}>{`Use Standard HTML Elements if you:`}</h4>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Need highly custom UI components`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Have specific design requirements beyond Spectrum`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Are comfortable writing custom CSS`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Want maximum control over styling and behavior`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`For most projects, `}<strong parentName="p">{`we recommend starting with Spectrum Web Components`}</strong>{`, and using standard HTML elements for very specific custom needs. UXP Widgets are still supported but may be deprecated in the future.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text, text2" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Creative Cloud Marketplace and Visual Language`}</p>
    <p>{`You are free to use your own visual language in UXP plugins. At the moment, products sent for approval to the Creative Cloud Marketplace don't need to implement the Adobe Spectrum Design System, although we recommend it for consistency.`}</p>
    <p>{`Best UX practices, as well as adherence to the Adobe Brand Guidelines, `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/distribution/review-guidelines/index.md"
      }}>{`will be enforced`}</a>{` during the approval process.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "working-with-javascript-frameworks"
    }}>{`Working with JavaScript Frameworks`}</h2>
    <p>{`While vanilla JavaScript, HTML, and CSS are sufficient for many plugins, complex applications can benefit from modern JavaScript frameworks. UXP plugins `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/tutorials/udt-deep-dive/plugin-workflows.md#working-with-bundlers"
      }}>{`work well`}</a>{` with popular frameworks like `}<strong parentName="p">{`React`}</strong>{`, `}<strong parentName="p">{`Vue`}</strong>{`, and `}<strong parentName="p">{`Svelte`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`These frameworks help you manage complex state, create reusable components, and build more maintainable code. However, they require additional setup including Node.js, package managers, and build tools.`}</p>
    <p>{`If you're already familiar with React or plan to build a complex plugin, check out the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-spectrum/Spectrum%20UXP%20Widgets/Using%20with%20React.md"
      }}>{`Using Spectrum with React`}</a>{` guide for tips on integrating Spectrum components with React. The most popular approach is to use `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://opensource.adobe.com/spectrum-web-components/using-swc-react/"
      }}>{`React Wrappers for Spectrum Web Components`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "reference-documentation"
    }}>{`Reference Documentation`}</h2>
    <p>{`Ready to start building? Explore the complete documentation for each UI approach.`}</p>
    <ul>

    <p>{`For practical examples and working code, explore the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
